contest.title: 2025-2026 ICPC Asia Taichung Regional Contest (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Sliding tiles with barriers; compute accumulated column answers after operations.
Sweep-line writeup maintains row states after inserting barriers and blocks using Fenwick and historical segment tree structures.

annotation.summary: Maintain the effect of sliding tiles around inserted barriers and aggregate how many tiles end in each column.
annotation.constraints: n up to several hundred thousand, so each update must touch only logarithmic data-structure nodes rather than all tiles between barriers.
annotation.core_idea: Scan rows from top to bottom and maintain, for each current barrier interval, how many blocks occupy the leading positions after sliding. Inserting a barrier splits an interval and rewrites prefix counts in the two new intervals; inserting a block sets one position in the appropriate interval. Fenwick trees and historical/range-sum segment structures maintain counts and accumulated column answers.

solution_variants.name: sweep-line-interval-data-structures
solution_variants.summary: Process events by row, split intervals at barriers, and maintain prefix occupancy with Fenwick/segment trees.

tags.evidence: The solution scans events row by row and updates the active interval structure.
tags.description: Process geometric or ordered events in one direction while maintaining active state.
tags.created_reason: Sweep line is a reusable paradigm not represented by current tags.

tags.evidence: Fenwick trees maintain counts/ranks inside barrier intervals.
A range/historical segment tree maintains accumulated column sums under range assignments.
tags.description: Use segment tree variants for range queries, range updates, persistence, or historical sums.
tags.created_reason: Segment tree is a core data structure category missing from the tag hierarchy.

tags.evidence: The official tag indicates DSU-style interval/next-pointer maintenance can be part of the implementation.
